name: workflows
context: Run reproducible pandas workflows for profiling, conversion, cleaning, transforms, joins, aggregations, and time-series resampling
info: This skill packages pandas as an executable local data workflow surface. It gives agents deterministic scripts for profiling unknown datasets, converting file formats, joining files, aggregating groups, transforming rows and columns, and resampling timestamped data. Use it when a task needs action, not only explanation: inspect data quality, produce a cleaned artifact, convert CSV to Parquet, merge exports, summarize metrics, or generate resampled time buckets. The single `SKILL.md` in this directory teaches the full pandas ecosystem and references the subnodes [[pandas/workflows/io]], [[pandas/workflows/clean]], [[pandas/workflows/transform]], [[pandas/workflows/join]], [[pandas/workflows/groupby]], [[pandas/workflows/time-series]], and [[pandas/workflows/performance]].
body:
# Pandas Workflows

This is the executable pandas node. It contains one Agent Skills `SKILL.md`, reusable scripts, and a reference file so agents can act without rewriting boilerplate.

## Install

```bash
python -m pip install 'pandas[performance,excel,parquet]'
```

## Script surface

```bash
# Profile rows, dtypes, missing values, duplicate rows, samples, and top values
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/profile_dataset.py data/orders.csv --parse-dates created_at

# Convert formats while preserving typed data better than CSV
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/convert_dataset.py data/orders.csv data/orders.parquet --parse-dates created_at

# Merge two datasets and validate cardinality
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/join_datasets.py users.csv orders.csv joined.parquet --on user_id --how left --validate one_to_many --indicator

# Aggregate by groups
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/aggregate_dataset.py orders.csv by_status.csv --by status --agg amount:sum --agg order_id:nunique

# Transform rows and columns safely
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/transform_dataset.py raw.csv clean.parquet --query 'amount > 0' --drop-duplicates

# Resample timestamped rows
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/resample_timeseries.py events.csv daily.csv --date-column ts --freq D --agg event_id:count
```

## Operating sequence

1. Profile first with [[pandas/workflows/io]] and [[pandas/workflows/clean]] signals.
2. Decide which dtypes and date parsing rules must be explicit.
3. Transform and clean into a new artifact; never mutate raw inputs.
4. Use [[pandas/workflows/join]] with `validate=` for combined data.
5. Use [[pandas/workflows/groupby]] or [[pandas/workflows/time-series]] for summaries.
6. Use [[pandas/workflows/performance]] if memory usage, runtime, or file size becomes a blocker.

## Outputs

Scripts write JSON summaries to stdout for agents and write data artifacts to requested output paths. Prefer Parquet for internal artifacts, CSV for exchange with humans or legacy tools, and JSONL when downstream systems consume records.

sections.title: Profile before changing data
sections.body: Run `profile_dataset.py` before writing transformation code. Check row count, column names, dtypes, missing percentages, duplicate rows, memory usage, sample rows, numeric summaries, and top values. Use the profile to decide dtype conversions, required fields, join keys, and whether the dataset fits in memory.

sections.title: Artifact policy
sections.body: Keep raw inputs untouched. Write derived artifacts to explicit paths such as `clean.parquet`, `joined.parquet`, or `summary.csv`. Parquet is the default for repeatable typed intermediate outputs because it preserves column types better than CSV and supports column pruning. CSV is acceptable for final human review.

sections.title: Script contract
sections.body: Each script accepts paths and options, writes result files only when requested, and emits a machine-readable JSON summary to stdout. This makes the scripts safe for agents to compose in shell automation and CI. Scripts fail fast on unsupported file formats, invalid join cardinality, bad aggregation specs, or missing optional dependencies.

sections.title: Handoffs to other nodes
sections.body: Use [[python/venv]], [[python/pip]], or [[python/uv]] to create the environment. Use [[duckdb]] when SQL over large files is more appropriate. Use [[database/sql]] when translating groupby, filter, and join work into database queries. Use [[testing]] to add regression tests for cleanup logic.

links.context: Section defining the shared workflow script contract
Read, profile, and write tabular files
Repair missing values, dtypes, and duplicates
Filter, select, rename, sort, deduplicate, and reshape data
Merge and concatenate datasets with cardinality validation
Aggregate grouped data and produce summaries
Parse timestamps and resample time buckets
Fast Python environment and dependency management

related.note: Executable workflow layer for the pandas ecosystem
Scripts run under Python and require pandas installed
Use DuckDB when the workflow is mostly SQL scans and joins over large files
Data cleaning scripts should have regression tests for edge cases

scripts.name: Profile Dataset
scripts.description: Inspect a CSV, TSV, JSON, JSONL, Excel, or Parquet dataset and emit a JSON profile of dtypes, missingness, duplicates, samples, and summaries

scripts.name: Convert Dataset
scripts.description: Convert tabular datasets between CSV, TSV, JSON, JSONL, Excel, and Parquet with optional column selection and date parsing

scripts.name: Join Datasets
scripts.description: Merge two tabular datasets with pandas.merge, cardinality validation, suffix control, and optional source indicator

scripts.name: Aggregate Dataset
scripts.description: Group a dataset by columns and compute aggregations from JSON or column:function CLI specs

scripts.name: Transform Dataset
scripts.description: Apply safe common transformations: select, drop, rename, query, deduplicate, sort, and write the result

scripts.name: Resample Time Series
scripts.description: Parse a timestamp column, optionally group by entities, resample by a pandas offset alias, and aggregate values

output_path.description: Destination path for workflows that write data

options.description: Script-specific flags such as join keys, aggregations, parse dates, or output format

summary_json.description: Machine-readable script summary emitted to stdout

artifact_path.description: Output dataset path when the workflow writes one

rows.description: Rows processed or emitted when applicable

depends.reason: Install pandas and optional extras with pip
Use uv for fast reproducible Python environments

frontmatter.author: dojo-community
frontmatter.audience: AI agents, data engineers, research engineers
